summary: The main claim is: we (humans) are successfully exterminating chimps even though it is actually illegal to kill chimps by human law!
But I think those responses are probably wrong, because you don't actually need "magical" technologies to end the world.
The main claim is: I am not suggesting that intelligence directly leads to the extinction of other species.
Our intelligence, relative to other animal species, can be classified as superintelligence.
The main claim is: The highest-level GFlowNet actor is trained on open-ended plans generated by the exemplary actor.
In section 4, I discuss the properties of the proposed H-JEPA agent with GFlowNet actors.

top_comment.author: Seth Herd
top_comment.text:
Given your summary in the other comment:

[...]

My question is: if we already have an aligned LMCA, why would we use it to train a less interpretable H-JEPA AGI? Including this in the top level summary would be really useful for explaining the potential significance of your proposal.

To me, the whole notion that an H-JEPA architecture would save us is highly counterintuitive. As Steve Byrnes explained in detail, LeCun's brainlike H-JEPA AGI architecture proposal really doesn't contain an alignment proposal. Sure, it could be aligned if you got the rules in its decision component just right. But that's just restating the whole alignment problem, which of course LeCun merrily assumes is actually easy - apparently without bothering to think about it.

So perhaps the significance here is: if LeCun's proposed architecture turns out to work well, we'd want a way to align it, so here's a proposal for doing it.
top_comment.claim: The comment's point: So perhaps the significance here is: if LeCun's proposed architecture turns out to work well, we'd want a way to align it, so here's a proposal for doing it.

summary: The main claim is: I expect most alignment researchers would agree that humans are in fact very messy.

Putting humans in a box and letting them deliberate about the problem for thousands of years I think this would be cool, but in my view, having an AI which is able to reliably do this and not mess it up requires a lot of alignment to begin…
The main claim is: I've been wondering about whether or not GPT-4 can solve fictional mysteries (or provide a best guess).
The main claim is: First one (arguably what we see right now with latest NNs) - super resource access, but not necessary super skills.
The main claim is: So I basically think that the core of the alignment problem, at the moment, is inner alignment + deceptive alignment.
Is the failure mode describe in Ajeya’s Without Specific Countermeasures an inner alignment failure, or an outer alignment failure (I think it’s both).
The main claim is: Even if I think we'll all die painlessly, how can I look at my children and not already be mourning their death from day 1?

ML Safety lacks good benchmarks, so the Center for AI Safety is offering $50,000 - $100,000 prizes for benchmark ideas (or full research papers).

The main claim is: An agent will logically have fewer resources at its disposal than the overall system, and to provide the benefit of leverage, the number of agents should be significant.

While deception by the mesa-optimizer ("agent") must be addressed, both humans and the AGI agree that agents going rogue to take actions that fulfill their sub-goal but thwart the overall mission must be prevented.
The main claim is: ^ “Yak shaving” means performing a seemingly endless series of small tasks that must be completed before the next step in the project can move forward.
